stories.title: Buckley resident sets up chair aerobics class for older neighbours
stories.summary: A former fitness instructor has set up a weekly chair aerobics class for residents at a Buckley over-55s housing scheme. Ann Walker, who moved to Nant Mawr Court with her husband Steve, started the class to give her neighbours a chance to keep mobile and meet up. Around eight residents take part each week. Nant

stories.title: More than 1,000 people a week in north Wales placed on suspected cancer pathway, data shows
stories.summary: More than 1,000 people a week in north Wales are being placed on a suspected cancer pathway, according to the latest figures from the Welsh Government. In the 12 months to February 2026, 52,619 patients in the Betsi Cadwaladr University Health Board area entered a pathway after a GP or other clinician suspected they might

stories.title: HMRC writing to thousands of 21-year-olds about forgotten Child Trust Funds
stories.summary: Thousands of 21-year-olds across the UK are to be written to by HM Revenue and Customs, reminding them they have a forgotten Child Trust Fund worth on average £2,200. The Government announced the campaign on 23 April. More than 750,000 Child Trust Fund accounts remain unclaimed, according to figures from HM Treasury and HMRC. Child

stories.title: Police appeal for help finding missing 15-year-old last seen at Deeside Leisure Centre
stories.summary: North Wales Police have appealed for help to find a missing 15-year-old last seen at Deeside Leisure Centre on Thursday evening. Officers said they had growing concerns for Jaden, who was last seen at the leisure centre at 8.30pm on 23 April. The force said it believed he was currently in the Flint area, but
